text: Worst company EVER.  I called to inquire about moving my cell service from Verizon to T-Mobile.  There were multiple things I needed to do, but the fist thing was to give them my credit card number so they could bill me one dollar in order to set up auto pay - later decided not to change my service yet $450 was taken from my credit card, and it took a month to get it corrected.  NIGHTMARE/FRAUDGELENT

T-Mobile Store #4139(144 Moreland Ave ) Had the most Genuine and Outstanding customer service thus far. Hollis & Keem did a great job helping me and was very knowledgeable about their work. I salute their GREAT customers service and helping one another out. I will come back to this one. Thank You Guys

Years back I would absolutely recommend t mobile, but now.DONT WASTE YOUR MONEY. They do not care about loyalty or customer care AT ALL. If you have a problem,it is yours. I have been promised so many solutions by them, I've been promised follow up phone calls by them, I've been promised credits by them, and absolutely none of which have been followed up by them. Save your time save your money and save your frustration and I highly recommend a different phone company

If I could put a negative star I will definitely do so. We have had T-Mobile service for over 2 years now. Had very bad experience with 2 Samsung cell phones we bought from them, an S23 being the last one.
Piece of advice, be very cautious with T-Mobile when they use the words "offers" and "free equipment". They are a complete rip off. They work under a full lying scheme. And when you look up for help, nobody solves. Also would not recommend the "autopay" with them.

- I saw a commercial on tv indicating that TMobile is willing to pay off your phones with your current carrier (up to 800) and bring you over to T Mobile. 
- I called to get my phone and three of my sons phones moved over to TMobile and luckily all of us owed under 800 for our phones.
- Spoke with a representative for two and a half hours on the phone doing all the transferring - after asking one crucial question - "Do I need to pay anything out of pocket?  Do I need to reach out to my current carrier to cancel my cell service."  - "No Ma'am - nothing is paid out of pocket - T Mobile handles everything and they will reach out to your carrier and do the transfer.  All you need to do is call us when you receive the sim cards in the mail (in 3 days) so we can get those activated for you.  We will call your carrier and complete the process, and all you need to do is call your current provider about cancelling your wifi service only after you get our TMOBILE one and hook it up and it works, then you call your carrier to cancel wifi and the process will be complete."
- Was also told I would receive an email upon completion of our call with instructions as well as promotional money on a gift card.  EMAIL NEVER CAME.
- 3 days later - sims come in the mail and I call indicating to the service rep that I never received the email with instructions so "I hope I'm doing this right"... and then told them that I wanted to know how to begin the process for T Mobile to pay off my phones with my current carrier.
- The rep tells me that I am supposed to pay my phone off and then T Mobile will send me a rebate for each phone.  I'm now dumbfounded as I spent 2 and a half hours on the phone getting everything prepared only to find that I am in fact now expected to pay out of pocket.  This rep tells me that this is T Mobile policy and that they wouldn't have said that they would pay it - and I said TRUST ME I would not have spent so much time on the phone setting up service for four phones without confirming what my expectations were for finances... and I told them that their rep not only told me I'd pay nothing out of pocket, but also told me emails would be coming that never came, the entire thing was not adding up.  
-  I asked to be done with them - without even ever getting started.  Asked for a shipping label and within 1 day I was at UPS returning the WIFI router and the sim cards, and I was washing my hands with t mobile... very disappointed with the entire thing.
-  within about a week or so - I get a charge on my debit card for 180.00 listed as a recurring charge for T MOBILE.  Now I'm back on the phone with t mobile for another hour while they "look into why I was charged and was told "We will look into this to see if we can do anything to get you a refund." 
- Rep gets back on the phone after a hold to tell me that they are processing the refund today but that it won't show up for three business days.  When I asked why was I charged when everything was immediately returned, I already cancelled, and never even had a second of t mobile service and their explanation was just that "this was the card you provided so it automatically got charged."  
- When I asked if there was anything they were willing to do to preserve my interest in T Mobile or to make up for the headache that I've had with them, with no service to show for it... and she said "this is business and there is nothing more we can do but process your refund." 
- They told me to have a wonderful day .... I did... BUT... I think anyone should consider this story when calling them.

March 2023, I went in the store to trade in my phone. I've had the phone for several years and decided it was time for a trade in. The employee, Sam, checked and determined I was elgible for a upgrade and $400 trade in value towards another phone. After hearing this I decided to trade in my phone. I noticed on my receipt I was only giving $25. I questioned Sam about this and he stated the $400 will reflect on my next bill. After receiving my bill I didn't get the quoted $400. I called customer service and the represented was very professional and agreed I should have been giving the $400 trade in value because I did qualified. I was instructed to go to the store and speak with the manager. I did but she wasn't there. Another employee tried his best to assist me and stated I was elgible because of the plan I was on at the time of the special. I left the store and returned three hours later. The manager was there but was very nonchalant about the error. She told me I was not elgible. If I've known that at the first visit, I would have kept my old phone. She said I wouldn't be able to use it. It dosent matter because it was mine and paid for. I left the store and called customer service again. The representative was very sympathic and understanding of my complaint. He was able to credit my bill $186. But still wasn't able to give me what I was quoted. I was grateful for what he did but still wanted what I was quoted. I am a $20 year customer with a family and business account. I can't believe this is how loyal customers are treated!

I was with Sprint for over 10 years. never got cut off or throttled. Now, 12 days late, get a message telling me I am cut off and will have to pay this month + $20. The message also said I owed 2 months. The 2nd month isn't even due for another 2 and a half weeks!
They throttle the speed a lot.
Seriously thinking Verizon or AT&T may be better.

Lied about EVERYTHING when signing up for their Magenta Max plan. I was told I was getting a new phone for free. NOT TRUE, after having this for about a year I just learned that if I switch carriers I have to pay for the phone. That's not what free means. I was told that the iPhone my wife traded in was an even trade for the new iPhone they gave us. ANOTHER LIE! Just found out these crooks only credited us $135 for her trade in. And we have to pay in full for that phone too. Also wad told no contract, cancel service any time. The "executive response" guy I just talked to informed me that we are in a 2 year contract currently. Canceling my service would therefore cost me HUNDREDS OF DOLLARS! On top of all this they lied about the price of the plan. It was supposed to be $140/mo. with EVERYTHING INCLUDED. And it has been up until now. Now they are going to raise my bill by at least $15/month. LIARS, CHEATS, CRIMINALS!!! There should be a class action lawsuit against these parasites!

I had prior bad experience of unauthorized charges on my account back in 2006. In 2018 my fiancé had t-mobile and i convinced him to leave. I paid off his balance in full and we transferred his service to another provider. About a month later they tried to charge him for services even though everything had been paid. We filed a BBB Report and got the problem rectified, or so we thought. Fast forward to now & we are trying to change our phone plan from a different company. Come to find out at some point t-mobile decided to lock our phone & when we called about it they proceeded to say we owed a balance. They refused to give the date the balance was assessed. Only threatened that they would not unlock our phone unless we paid the balance due. I explained to them that no balance is due and we even have the BBB Report # to prove it. They were extremely dismissive and pretty much told us to pay the balance or we don’t get our phone unlocked. I have now filed another complaint with the BBB to again rectify this issue. NEVER USE/TRUST T-Mobile! They are the biggest scam artist company I have ever had the displeasure of having to interact with. Stay away from them at all costs.

I was literally at my wit's end and ready to break down and cry.  An update locked my phone (I had never locked it, therefore no password set up to unlock).  After being on another phone for 5 hours trying to get help, I was told repeatedly I would have to do a factory reset and lose everything.  I did the reset, but Samsung wanted to text me a code to unlock the reset, but no text messages could get through.  I took the phone to the Princeton, Texas T-Mobile store, and Pedro had personality plus, but he also had lots of knowledge and got my phone operative.  Then he restored all my contacts, photos, and calendar.  Super kudos to Pedro.  You definitely deserve a raise!
During the hardest economical times Tmobile adds thier name to another company that wants a raise. I'm looking into going to verizon just because of this.

I am writing this in regards to the Carbondale, Pa store only. I went into the store to purchase 2 phone lines and told the Peter the store personnel that I would eventually purchase a 3 rd line after my prepaid service with trac phone ended. I didn’t want to lose the 200.00 I just paid for tracphone. He then came up with this deal but 2 lines get one free. So I told him I was a first responder and wanted to know the price for the buy 2 lines get one free with first responder and go 5 g plan. He told me 90.00 for the 3 lines. Since I was getting the 3 rd line free I decided to go for that plan and lose out on tracphone. I also wanted to get a new phone for my daughter only but once again he had found another promo of buy and phone get 700.00 off second phone so I went for it and got myself an iPhone13 even though not needed but it still was an upgrade for me. So now ready to go over monthly what this will cost me. He gave me a piece of paper and I started writing everything down line by line. 
90.00 for 3 lines with the one free with first responder 
38.75 monthly for my daughters phone 
1.25 for my phone
40.00 for the internet which I already had but with purchasing phone lines it brought the internet from 50.00 with auto pay to 40.00
So grand total was 170.00 per month!!
I asked him several times if this was correct and he kept telling me over and over and laughing that yes he promises me it’s correct within one or two dollars. If not he even gave me his personal cell number and he would fix it if it wasn’t correct. 
Well fast forward just about a month and my bill is over 230.00!! I called T-Mobile and was told Peter from the Carbondale PA store gave me wrong info. That the promo for the 3 rd line free wasn’t for first responders!! I called Peter and he said he never knew I was first responder til the end. Not True I gave him my first responder ID as soon as we sat down together. LOOK ON THE STORE CAMERA!!! Besides that if that is true why didn’t he tell me at the end I still wouldn’t have signed anything and would have walked away. So I talked to the assistant store manager DENISE and she just lied as well saying Peter didn’t know I was first responder yet she wasn’t even at the store when I was there!!!!! I asked her to work it out where I pay what I was quoted and she said no way can she do that and she is sorry. I told her sorry doesn’t cut it. I made this purchase as to how much I could budget each month. She said she will talk to the manager at the store and never got back to me. I did call T-MOBILE headquarters and was told they will honor the 90.00 per month. They did credit my first bill but I am still waiting to see how my second bill goes. Hoping not to have to call and explain all this over again!!! Bottom line is do not go to the Carbondale PA store and deal with Peter Saad or Denise!!! Peter needs more training to make sure no other customer’s are lied to and then blindsided when they get the bill
Do not port your number to T-mobile or any of their subsidiaries, they still won't give me correct port number to leave. Been 6 days of daily calling.
T-Mobile implemented new payment requirements, requested checking account numbers, Just wondering how T-Mobile is going to protect the accounts since they was hacked several times already ,

I was a Sprint customer for 13 years and never had trouble with service if I paid a bill late (once I was over 1000 dollars behind). I go through some changes as a result of my health and suddenly I am threatened by text and my service is suspended. I never wanted T-MOBILE and just because they bought out Sprint I have been forced to get a new phone and AWFUL SERVICE and customer service. It's worth it to me to switch to Verizon or AT&T JUST to not have to deal with them. If you're smart, you'll switch too.

This is in reference to T-Mobile and Assurant cramming their customers. On May 30, 2023, $731.61 was debited out of my Chase Account for my T-Mobile cellphone bill payment of which 299.66 was for a fee. The fee was a non return fee for not returning a cellphone device. I have been charged this fee previously on Feb 2022 and was assured by T-Mobile it was a mistake and wouldn’t happen again. So on May 30 when my autopay went through, I was very upset to see it had happen again. The phone was shipped on April 10, 2023 and received by T-Mobile on April 17, 2023. I receipt of this transaction from USPS. I called T-Mobile and I was told I needed to call Assurant since they are the ones who charged the $299.66 return fee. I was also told by the T-Mobile rep that text messages and alerts would have been sent to my phone letting me know that I was going to be charged a 299.66 non return fee. I received no messages to my phone line . I also told the rep there were no messages sent to my phone or on the phone line executing the insurance claim. She assured me there were messages sent and I assured her she could look in her system to see there were no messages sent. I was told that I had to call Assurant if I wanted to dispute the charge. So I called Assurant and was told they received the phone on April 17th but it was never scanned in. I was told I would receive an account credit in 1-2 billing cycles. I told the rep that was unacceptable. I didn’t pay them in bill credits, they took cash from my account. I asked for a refund and she said she couldn’t do it. I would receive my credit in 1-2 billing cycles. I asked to speak to a Manager and his name was Dan or Dave. The Manager at Assurant told me and it is also in T-Mobile’s notes that there must have been a glitch in the system. He repeated this lie several times. He could not understand why I was charged when the phone was in their position since April 17th but it wasn’t t scanned into the warehouse and he didn’t t know why. I told the Manager I wasn’t going to wait for my money when the charge wasn’t an authorized charge and I wasn’t informed of this charge. So the Manager as Assurant called T-Mobile while I was on the line to see if I could get my refund sent to me directly. I was told by the T-Mobile rep that I would receive my refund in 5 days and that she would call me to follow up. No one called to follow up. The credit went to my account. On June 3rd, I filed a complaint with BBB and also filed a dispute with my bank as well. Chase Bank returned 299.66 to my account while they investigated the charge. On June 6th, T-Mobile refunded my account as well. I got a call from John Rankin on the Executive Response Team that my account was now past due and I needed to make a payment. I made a payment of $282.55 to bring my account current. So I essentially paid an unauthorized payment twice. I have receipts from the previous fee they charged me on Feb 2022 and now again May 30, 2023. I spoke to a T-Mobile rep around June 15 because I was upset that Chase sent the $299.66 payment back to T-Mobile. I asked for my refund again and I didn’t receive it. I was told by the rep and I have a screen shot of the conversation: “The reason why you get charge for non-return fee before you return the device is because your bill generated already before you sent back the device.” I told the rep that you can’t do that, that’s cramming. I am not some new disgruntled customer. I stuck it out with T-Mobile since June of 2005. I came back from a deployment to Kuwait and T-Mobile was one of my first stops. I was in Kuwait a year and I just wanted to connect with my family. I have stayed through a lot of bad times but this was unacceptable. I have been a customer 18 years and on Autopay with 12 lines. I paid on time every month! The service is bad, calls are constantly failing and they do nothing about it. The government just keeps fining them, no accountability.

This has to be the most frustrating thing that happened to me.
Too bad I realized TMobile is so bad after 10 years of being with them and now that I switched, I feel relieved.
The problem is as simple as it could be, just requesting this TMobile phone to be unlocked, has been paid off 3 months ago even the final balance as well.
Unlocking a phone, I am aware that can only be done through calling their customer service. I did not take the names initially because I thought it will be easy.

July 30, called to request for phone unlock. Did that manual unlock, stating Error: 1019, representative aware. Was told wait for 72 hours. 

August 4, followed up, was told to wait again for 72 hours, and that an email will be received. No email received in all inbox including Spam folder. Representative aware, of Error 1019. I mentioned phone was already reset/rebooted, has been on the whole time, it is connected to wifi. Another provider sim card was tried out to check.

August 7, called again, and stated it has been 8 days ago since initial request and nothing was resolved yet. Representative said issue will be escalated to resolutions department and will be prioritized, wait for 24 hours and will send an email. Again, not solved and no email received.

August 10, my 4th call in 11 days, spoke with Diane. I asked to speak to supervisor but representative said supervisor was engaged in another call, instead tried to do all the things the past 3 representatives did, which I cooperated again and again. I am not even yelling, but already frustrated at this time. I requested to speak to a technical support, and still was unable to help and was told for again, issue will be escalated, will inform the higher managers, wait for 72 hours. Total time of call was 1.5 hours with that 0.5 hours (30 mins) of being on hold to wait for that transfer to technical support. Saying it will be surely unlocked since it is already prioritized and rest assured it will be resolved.

August 11, after 24 hours, still not resolved. I went to TMobile store at the mall, stated they cannot help unlock a phone which I am aware but what more can I do if I can’t be helped at the only place where they say has the job to do that? I mentioned the whole story and my frustration and was told to go to this other TMobile store because it is the Corporate office so I went there, and I asked this guy if he can call for me. He said just call and say I am activating a line to get in the call faster. I said the problem is not getting to talk to someone but “the problem is not being solved for almost 2 weeks and I keep making this calls, waiting to talk to someone, hoping someone can find the problem and I have been very cooperative and patient, and kept being promised but the issue has like nowhere to go”. And he said he cannot make the call, that it has to be me…
I went back home and called the customer service line for the 5th time, spoke with Lou, and I asked to speak to a supervisor immediately. Of course, they see the note, and she said she is very knowledgeable on unlocking phones, and tried to do another way which was unsuccessful again. I cooperated and I know she is just trying to solve it and not transfer the call anymore to the supervisor but seeing it still did not work, I repeated myself and said, please, let me talk to a higher one, and so I was transferred to the manager, some wait time again, and so I spoke with Christian, said is one of the managers there. Same thing again and again, telling me issue will be escalated, I do not know how many escalations it should go when this is my 5th call. He tried to cut me short because I am already so frustrated telling me “Rest assured Maam, it will be unlocked in 24 hours”. The exact same thing Diane, the representative I spoke to yesterday, said. I asked, “if this is still unlocked, who else should I talk to? Should I call and look for you?”.. and just said the same thing, “rest assured…” I’m getting tired of the promises. 

2 days passed and I am already giving up because they will just keep me waiting for another 24 hours, my precious time has been wasted too much. I wanted to call that manager for him to know, but my husband stopped me cause I already am stressing on this for half a month. I believe there is a problem and I was cooperating and willing to find out the problem, but I never got the help I needed and it’s more frustrating that I do not know where to go anymore because even in person, I felt I was declined to be helped. This is TMobile’s service to former customers.

Stopped by this store in late May to get a monthlt prepaid service for the two new cell phones I've just purchased for family. I asked for the cheapest monthy pre-paid phone plan and I was told it's $25  each for the cheapest plan plus another $25 each for initiation.  I went home and found out this store has LIED TO ME about the cheapest plan as it's only $10 per phone instead of $25!!! I've called and complained and received no refunds!!!  I've decided to switched to $10 per month plan after the first month for another two months and both line were cutoff the services after only one month regardless I've already paid for another two months. I called the T-Mobile Pre-Paid services and got put on hold for ~10 min before I can speak to anyone. It took another 20+ minutes for someone to understand the situation and turn both phones back on without any explaination as why they did this.
TERRIBLE LIES AND PREPAID PHONE SERVICES FROM T-MOBILE. DO NOT USE THEM!!!  THEIR CHARGE OF THE PRE PAID PHONE INITATION FEES ARE ALSO RIDICULOUS!!!
doesn't matter who I'm on the phone with -- call cuts out. in the middle of civilization.

it takes 50 to 60 minutes to get someone on the phone. English isn't their native language so they're hard to understand. Today an hour and a half was wasted with wrong transfers and long hold times. Billing issue not resolve last 4 month since change plan. every time have to call for credit.

I bought  a phone from tmobile  and paid for it in full ...but because i dont want they service any more ....they locked a 1000 dollar phone ....and telling me ..i need to pay the  the account.... TMOBILE IS A RIP OFF ...ANYBODY I  KNOW I WILL NOT TELL THEM TO GO TO TMOBILE

I am with Visible now but want to change. After reading all the bad reviews it scares me to even think of switching to T-Mobile. It is ashame in this day and time to be able to find a company that treats its paying customer right. Total greed!

I’ve been with T-Mobile for 17 years and am blown away by these comments. I’ve zero issues but I never miss a bill. I had gotten payment arrangements maybe twice with no problems. I love using their service and have used Verizon and sprint as well. I use customer service maybe 5-8 times a year and never once did I not get something resolved. I hate that everyone’s having issues….

Absolutely horrible service in many ways. Things rarely work properly and when you try online service, they tell you they can't do anything and you need to go to a store. Then when you go to the store, they tell you that only online  service can help. 3 hours wasted today alone.

My family and I switched to T-Mobile because we had Metro that was bought out by T-Mobile and with Metro it’s harder to get a decent phone and I got greedy. When we first signed up we were promised a promotional credit of $800 which sold me. I never received it despite many hours of fighting customer service for it. It was my “switching” bonus which got denied because we were with a company they bought. I specifically asked the representative if that would be a problem and he said no. Our second problem came when we booked hotels through T-Mobile Tuesday. They were originally meant as rest stops between driving to see family. Well we ended up moving to the state with that family and cancelled the booking. We were told no refund would be made even though it was months in advance. I understood because the original booking said no refunds. However, I was still receiving emails telling me about my upcoming trip and how to check in so I assumed it didn’t get cancelled since I didn’t get a refund. We had a falling out with my family so we were going to use the hotel as a rest stop coming home, only to find out it was cancelled and basically I couldn’t do anything about it. I fought for hours and finally after a few days received an email saying I’m getting the refund, only to find out the account the refund is going to is closed because I moved state. I am now fighting with them to get the refund in a different way and it’s constant disorganization. One will tell me they’re putting in a request and wait 2 days, another will tell me they have no control and I need to contact the hotel and another will tell me to contact the bank I no longer have an account for. They are disorganized and I wish I never switched. Learn from my mistakes and NEVER switch.

Left on hold for an hour and 11 minutes on my seventh customer service rep and we conveniently got disconnected!!!!! I never got my rebate and I canceled a prepaid plan with zero dollars due and now they suddenly came up with a bill two months later for $112. First of all, I never got the rebate they promised in their home Wi-Fi deal and second of all it’s a prepaid plan I didn’t know anything and I even have the receipt that says zero dollars. Total scam total nightmare.
I'm so excited about the 5- star service received by Sir Cartier at the Crossroads location in Terrell, Texas. I wish more employees at Tmobile would follow Cartier's lead of Good Service.

The price that are charge to have their service is high enough. Not to mention it isn't as good as they claim it is. On top of that they are now charging you to pay your bill if you go into a store to pay it, you will pay an extra $5.00. If you call and talk to some one to pay your bill it is an extra $10.00. This is BS. They get enough money out of people and now they want more to get paid. Dumbest BS I have ever heard. They don't consider the elderly that barely know how to work their phone much less know how to use an app to pay their bill. They don't care. People on fixed incomes and they just want more and more for not the greatest service.
We rented the Tmobile home internet box. And decided to use it for our camper on vacation. Worked out, it was great. We will take it again. Thanks T Mobile.

Yesterday I went to TMobile at V.R.C. because I was only getting WI FI service and couldn't use my phone if I got away from my house...I just figured I might email accidently pushed a button or something. So the guy looks at the phone. It was on at the time, he opens it up , I wasn't really paying attention to what he did but when he was done my phone was completely dead and wouldn't turn on or even charge. It wasn't dead when I got there and the battery was at 56%. I definitely needed a phone but I felt completely hustled!! Then while he's waiting on me right in the middle of it starts helping someone else...just leaves me hanging there!! That's sloppy service. I need to go back in today and have them open it!! I wanna see what he disconnected!! For the most part we have had tmobile for over 15 years and I like it just fine.... but yesterday was shocking!!

I cannot rate the service, just the customer service.  I have been interested in trying T-Mobile instead of Verizon for the packages T-Mobile offers, as well as more universal 5G coverage.  I have spent SIX hours this weekend, in three separate chat sessions trying to get first answers then my phone numbers moved. You would think the sales dept would jump at an easy sale, but not so easy!  Each time I was on chat, latency between responses measured in the tens of minutes.  Each time, after 2 hours of painfully slow communications back and forth, I was forced to terminate conversation due to other duties.  This is absurd!  Each tech blamed software upgrade issues - not what I want to hear from a company that is supposed to provide COMMUNICATION SERVICES!  Phone lines were 'long wait'  I am not terribly pleased with the 5G coverage Verizon has, but really, the pain of converting to T-mobile may not be worth it!

T mobile is By far the worst decision I have ever made. Cricket wireless has better coverage than these scumbags! If you are considering switching don't it's not worth the aggravation. They're in breach a contract have never once delivered. The service I was promiand now won't let me transfer back to a reputable company. Without paying them a bunch of money that i've already paid for services not rendered properly. Do not I repeat do not ever go to t mobile they are the worst!!!! I only gave them one star because you couldn't give negative stars. The date at the bottom is the most recent experience. This has been going on for 10 months.

I would give 0 starts if I could.  I have been a TMobile customer for about 5 years and they have the WORST CORPORATE SUPPORT PROGRAM IN THE UNIVERSE.

Their employees and customers are underpaid, under supported and treated as disposable.  

TMobile set up my Autopay when I got my new phones which has been working perfectly for 2 years.  Now in order to squeeze a few more bucks out of me they want access to my checking account/debit card so they don't have to pay credit card fees.  Anyway their Autopay web site cratered when I tried to give them the demanded information.  Now I'm sitting on hold after being threatened that I will be billed $10 for just talking to customer service.  Best of all the bot keeps telling me that I have complete control by going to their WEB sit LOL.

WORST USE OF TECHNOLOGY AND HUMAN RESOURCES EVER.

First of all,(and worst of all) the Philippines reps are the worst ever to deal with of all countries that handle customer service. Then there's the over 20 minute wait to even get someone on the phone. Then the round and round, useless conversational tactics of the reps, constantly over trying to be polite and reassuring you they're doing there best to help you, while the whole time they've failed to address any issue at all. Zero help. They can't even refresh a signal in a call after being transferred 3 times and over an hour on the phone. Those Philippinos are the worst. Ever.

T-Mobile sucks! Im a 20+year customer in a bad place (out of work - no money). Called to see if I can receive incomimg calls until I can pay my bill and was immediately told no by Rep JT. Being loyal used to mean something back in the day and now it doesn't. When I get back on my feet I will be looking for a new provider!

We have been trying to work with T-Mobile for over 4 months for them to return credits for service that we were mischarged for.  When we initiated service a "dummy" line was created for the Digits feature.   Due to poor reception and even worse customer service we decided to switch providers.  We had called T-Mobile to ensure all lines were cancelled.  We were assured that the account was now closed.  Since that time we were charged for 2 full months of service.  Turns out that the rep "forgot" to cancel the dummy line, likely to retain the commission.  I had contacted customer service to remedy this situation and return the money to our account.  Customer service at T-Mobile is very well trained on how to be fake, nice actors.   For the past 4 months we have been given false promises, "personal guarantees" of call backs, told there would be trouble tickets made, etc.  No one keeps their word.  This seems to be T-Mobile policy.   I'm sure there are plenty of people out there who just "pay to get rid of the issue" - maybe this is the tactic of T-Mobile.  Annoy and anger people enough so that people give up and take their financial losses.  The local representatives are no different.  They are commission hungry, then drop you like a nuisance the moment there's an issue.  They refuse to handle customer service issues and will push you to customer service over the phone.  With customer service if you demand enough they will allow you to talk to a supervisor, who is more skilled at fake promises and guarantees.  Above that they will not allow you to talk to the manager above them.  It is also their policy that you cannot know who they are or have any contact information to follow up with them.  You will talk to someone once and then never again, so there is no accountability for them lying.  STAY AWAY!  T-mobile is full of thieves.  I feel horrible for their employees as I'm sure many of them are working in an unethical job in order to pay their bills.  May their tainted souls be saved.
Thomas had to be the best we ever had at the Butler Store. We need him to stay. He was the best!!!

Some of the worst service I have ever had! Phone says I have service and internet, but it rarely works. I constantly have to optimize my phone, toggle Airplane Mode, reset my network, pull the Sim Card, wipe cache partition, and reset my phone. And even after all that, it rarely fixes the issue.

The saddest part is, there are 3 T-mobile locations in the town I am in. I went into one location, to ask them. But, they had no idea about anything, and were completely unhelpful.

So basically, I pay for super spotty service, and terrible customer service.

And as the title says, T-mobile is not worth the headache. Especially if you rely on service to earn a living like I do.

Note: There is no specific date that the issue started, so I just picked a random one. All I know, it is getting much worse!

Also, I must mention that I even did a Factory Reset. It didn't solve the issue at all!

ONE STAR TOO MANY ! BEEN TO THE T-MOBILE STORE IN EPHRATA,PA.5 TIMES IN THE LAST 3 DAYS.MALCOM STILL CAN'T FIX THE ISSUES.  NEITHER COULD MARANDA OR THE OTHER INCOMPETENT. YET, MALCOLM TRIED SELLING ME JUST ABOUT EVERYTHING IN THE STORE. HEY, MALCOLM, YOU CAN'T EVEN GET MY 2 PHONES WORKING. TIME TO SWITCH CARRIERS.

Please bring T-Mobile support back to the USA!  For 20 years or more T-Mobile service was the best, I was able to get a rep on the phone in a few minutes and issues quickly resolved.  Now, with their overseas call center, it takes 30 to 50 minutes to get someone on the phone. English isn't their native language so they're hard to understand and seldom are able to do anything helpful.  Today an hour and a half was wasted with wrong transfers and long hold times.
It's impossible to get to anyone in customer service. They used to be pretty good but now they are terrible.
They took away the autopay discount because of paying with a CC. Penalizing customers is not good. I would actually give them no stars.

They referred an overdue account to collections. TM refuses to give my account information to me. They simply want me to pay without knowing anything about the bill. Debt collector said they can't help that I needed TM to resolve the issue.

Never ever use this company but most of all NEVER do payment arrangements because they won't honor that, I was charged the entire amount after 5 months of paying on time and even when I disputed the un-authorized amount I didn't win. They are horrible

Terrible company I tried back in may to get a prepaid account couldn't get one so I canceled cause my phone company wouldn't unlock my phone and they said they cancelled then a month later I was billed $55.39 I went back and forth with T-Mobile they asked for my phone number I'd never had a number with them and stuff they asked what number I was trying to transfer so I gave him the number that I was trying to transfer I gave him all my email addresses everything home address and my social security number and they said that oh none of the information they had on file none of it okay now it's been 3 months later 3 months later or 2 months later something like that and I get a bill for $200 and some dollars saying protect my credit blah blah blah and stuff and then I need to pay it off because my fee the bill whatever is saying that I was late and so they closed out my service my account so anyways we called T-Mobile we went back and forth for like two or three hours and everything just back and forth back and forth back and forth and stuff gave him the account number on the bill that they gave me oh they couldn't find anything well first they're like okay what's your login and all your information and stuff didn't know because I never created an account and stuff may I ask do you know your pen I said no I never created a pen I've never created an account so then they said okay well we can't get into the account but there's an account here so then they transfer us to another department same thing back and forth back and forth back and forth they couldn't get in and so then all of a sudden they transferred to somebody else bang for a bag and forth back and forth with them all sudden oh we can't find an account and data da duh that an account doesn't exist here and everything and yet houses and count doesn't exist when let's see I got a notice in the mail for $200 and some dollars that's owed and they took out $55.39 out of my bank account for an account this is crazy.
Trash company and trash support. Had to contact 4 different people won’t couldn’t help with my trial account being sent to collections affecting my credit score. No help whatsoever

We used the Willamette st store in Eugene OR. NONE of the promises we were made- low cost per monthly bill, low cost of phone trade ins, pay off of Att phones, cable bundle service - none of it has been accurate or true. Our bill is greater than before, the phone discounts and trade in were not as represented and the 5g service for cable was not available as promIsed. We have had to return over and over with complaints trying to get the plan promised. The reality is they outright lied like used car salesmen and 3 months later when the real bill hit nothing was accurate. New Phones amounts owed are >$800 not $200 as promised with trade in credits, trade in credits took 2 months not 2 weeks to arrive, bill is over $300 a month not the $180 estimated at time of contract switch. Bogus bait and switch tactics which should be illegal. Buyer bewarev!!
Same horrible service as Sprint plus my voicemail messages are received 24 hours later.

Tosha told us we got a free iPhone 14 upgrade as new customers with a trade-in.  We trusted her knowing what she was talking about. Turns out it was not free.  I would like to think she didn't lie on purpose, but her ignorance of the promo is still a big problem.  We did not need an upgrade.  We are now on the hook for an extra $21 a month for two years that we should not be paying.  Make sure you read the terms yourself and don't trust what they tell you.

I called T-Mobile Customer Service today as my phone locked up on me. In all of my past experiences with customer service, if I didn't have my PIN which I do not, they always asked me my security question. Today, this girl refused to do that. When I brought this up her reply was "Well, I don't know anything about that."  Why do some of their employees do one thing and others the opposite? Either you all do the same thing or you just don't do anything at all. I'm about to leave this company. Been with them for a while but it may be time to move on. They have always had the absolute worst customer service.

They are not trustworthy. Even though I canceled my subscription last April, 2023, they kept on charging my credit card. And their customer service is worse. All they want is your money. Don’t get fooled with their offer. Aside from poor connection, they robbed people. Even though I told them to cancel my subscription, they automatically deduct from my credit card, even though they saw not a call or text has been used. They are a greedy company.

My identity, WITHOUT PERMISSION, FALSELY USED by T Moble store Ruidoso, NM, customer service person, declaring FOR THEIRSELF 5 STARS on local Googled site, Saturday, July 22, 2023.  Reports in process to Corporate.  My time is too valuable to hassle with this.  Not returning to that store again, gotta go to a city store now, 3 hours' drive.  Incredibly bad and unethically crummy manners

This company is disaster. We have been a business client for ~10 years and every time they "upgrade" the system it gets worse. The recent one was done couple of months ago and we lost access to the portal altogether. After more than 10 calls and bazillion e-mails it is still not working. Please restore your system back to 2015 and fire all of your internal IT team. And 5G - another round of scam - yes we tested it, it has about 1/10 of our cable speed. How these guys not go to jail for perpetual lies and marketing scams/fraud?
I don't like the way you do business!

I would give 0 stars if I could. I live 30 minutes from Cincinnati & constantly drop service. Always get answered the tower in our town is being worked on & once it's fixed... it will be good. That's been going on over a year. We'll be switching soon. I'm done!! What can you do with a phone with no usable service?!

I have had phone and internet service with T-Mobile for 3 months now. I couldn't be any happier with them. I wen from not being able to use Comcast phone or 1 bar to 5 bars at almost all times. The internet service is just as good and I actually have faster download speeds. I can't say enough about how much I enjoy my T-mobile service, and I am saving $80.00 a month.

Literally the worst!  I have been on the line with them for over 3 hours and they still can't bundle a phone line and home internet.  I can't even hear the customer support because it's so loud.  She keeps telling me a typhoon is coming through!  T-mobile what is your problem?

Terrible website.  Everything is very slow, nothing works right, the links in the main menu disappear when you hover to it.  Pages just don't even open.  How could a tech company have such a terrible website and interface?

Really 0 stars. I was with sprint  made to change phone. Samsung a32  is a piece of junk. Didn't  last 2 years.  They want me to upgrade.  These phones are not cheap. So not upgrading.  Going back to home phone.  And save me more money.

I’ve literally had the worst T-mobile experience. Placed an order for an upgrade on 6/30. Still no phone nor a refund. These people have given me the runaround and NO ONE is on the same accord. They tell me they put a claim in, I call back and the new rep tells me their putting one in today when I was told it was done on the 13th of July; however it wasn’t done until today. I gave up and switched to AT&T because who has time to play these games and I let this company completely waste my time and energy for almost a month, but they sympathized with me lol.
There customer service is fxcking ridiculous I’m switching to another phone provider I’ve never waited an hour to get help over the phone.

Tmobile is the worst I’ve had in 20 years. They don’t care   The internet modem was so slow didn’t keep it. The free phone that gave me didn’t work. Now we had to pay $450 for the defective phone. They just suck. Never had any problems with any phone service until them

Cstomer Service has been fine to deal with, for me. But accordingly, TM's new policy on verification of identification has stopped me from getting new Sim card for new phone. 
 Just short intro about my experience where phone stolen over week ago. New one arrived today and I got new (replacement) Driver's License from DMV today as well. Went to present at TM and they couldn't scan it in, being the standard paper copy that serves as one's valid Driver's License until the actual card is received in the mail. (I use as my ID, the DL). Ok, and no i don't have passport or other issued Gov. ID. Unless or until i have actual "card" TM won't give me a Sim Card. Unbelievable.

I have the home internet service. Generally it is above the 100mbps level. For the last 3 days it struggles to be above 5mbps. The support team is helpless and the cust service doesn’t give a damn. I thought COX was bad but this service is UNACCEPTABLE.

Just don't! Unbelievable how rude customer service was. I canceled the same day we received out phones because of this and they still tried to bill me for 1 month payment. Terrible....terrible company

This is the worst phone company I ever dealt with customer service is terrible they leave you on hold for hours then when they finally answer its some one who doesn't speak English can't understand a word they say it's to pricey not worth the headache

T-Mobile is just a money scam
They steal the customers money every chance they get. Poor customer service and  worst company ever

OMG I WAS ON HOLD A TOTAL OF ONE HOUR AND 15 MINUTES, I KEPT GETTING TRANSFERRED WENT THROUGH HOOPS TO GET A TRANSFER PIN DID EVERYTHING THEY ASKED OF ME I WAS OUT AND DID NOT HAVE THE INFORMATION WITH ME, SO I WENT TO THE STORE SO THE STORE COULD VERIFY ME AND STILL WOULD NOT HELP ME OUT. MY PHONE SERVICE SUCKS AS WELL, TCL STYLUS IS A POS I WENT CHEAP FOR THE FIRST TIME NEVER AGAIN!   BUT THE CONNECTIVITY IS ATROCIOUS. AND THE HOME INTERNET DOESN'T WORK MOST OF THE TIME EITHER.

I am very thankful that Barbara G (Customer care) guided us patiently what to do and helped us to reach out  to the right T mobile store for help. The T-Mobile store in Desoto(851 I35 east) is very helpful especially Bryce and another lady. They were very patient and Bryce spent more than half an hour helping us to activate my new iPhone. Therefore, I want to say a big "thank you" to these lovely people.

I went to T mobile in Bullhead AZ to get a new phone. Rep said we had no credit with them even though i had their service for almost two years. Rep said if I bought a phone from Walmart she could transfer all my data in the cloud to new phone, as my old phone had been stolen. So I buy new phone, go back next day and she tells me T mobile will no longer transfer any data from a phone they did not sell me. Used the excuse that they would be liable if anything happened while transferring data! So I now have to pay for a phone line I have no access to as I refuse to pay for one of their overpriced phones! Run don't walk away from this company! This is only one of 3 incidents with this company and they never rectify the problems.

If I can give less than one star, I would. Just today I paid a $475 non-return fee for a device I had returned months ago. I had been reasured that I had returned it and have tracking proof that it has been returned. I was told that when the returned device was scanned in the fee would fall off. I was also told that My account would not be cut off but now it has. So I had to pay. When my phone is paid they will probably be a thing of the past.

I just got a text from them. They said as of August 7, 2023 they will not be excepting credit card as auto pay. They want your bank information. I talked with PNC, and they said absolutely do not give anybody your debit card or your routing number that goes directly into your bank accounts. I guess I’ll be moving on from T Mobil . They’re getting greedy they do not want to pay the fee that the credit card company charges them.

Store location: T Mobile at: The Galleria in Houston: 5135 W Alabama St. #6010 Houston TX 77056
WARNING: DO NOT GO TO THIS T-MOBILE. DO NOT GIVE THEM YOUR INFORMATION. 
I was at this store on 7/14/2023 @ 1pm. Wanted to get pricing on getting a new phone. Ask for my ID to pull up my account to give me more details. Was told will get good deal if I switch my current plan to a new one which will increase $75 a month. I told them I will talk to my son and will come back. Without my consent they switched my plan. SCAM SCAM SCAM. Had to call TMobile customer service for hours to fix this issue. Be careful who you trust and hope they did not use all my personal information on anything else.

T-Mobile service keep switching from 5g service to LTE service that’s doesn’t work the internet and it drops calls like crazy. It’s been doing this for months and I’m paying 198$ a month for this horrible service which suck! 
I been with T mobile 7 years and this year been extremely frustrating and horrible, just like a bad marriage, time for a divorce.
This company is a joke. They sell you one thing over the phone then bill you for something else.  I will never use there service again.  I would give no stars if I could

After inquiring about a new WIFI and mobile account I was told that I would qualify for a smart watch gift with the $99 fee waved and a $200 gift card for porting over from another cell company. After 2 months of glitchy WIFI reception from their wireless 5G box their technical support said I would not ever get a good signal because of my proximity to a lake. Then I called about the discount and gift card. I was then told I wasn't getting a discount as that was apparently to get me to sign up and I didn't qualify for the gift card. After spending several hours over several phone sessions I was told I did qualify for the gift card but would have to wait another month to receive it. I never got the $99 discount and after finally receiving the gift card I used it help pay the full retail ($289) for the lowest end smartwatch I didn't want. These are predatory practices with no obvious accountability or governing oversight. Consumer beware.

The WORST TELEPHONE COMPANY EVER MADE!!! Customers service is horrible , The service is the so bad . They tell you they are going to provide you with a service and give you a whole another one . DON’T GET T-MOBILE THEY SUCK!

I have their internet service.  It's working pretty well for me.  But once you sign up you will never talk to them again for follow up service/questions.  I have called several times in recent months and after being on hold for 20-30 minutes I gave up and hung up.  I simply do not have free time to waste like that.  Based on my income T-Mobile owes me hundreds of dollars in lost time.

Based on the negative reviews I read I take it these guys don’t give a crap about anyone besides themselves. I’m having numerous issues with my connection. Static, dropped calls, data issues. After 12 repeated calls to ask them to resolve these issues I’m still at square one and the service is getting worse not better. I asked them for a new device and the tech I had to deal with only referred me back to Apple even though I’m financing the purchase of my phone through them.  I hate their attitude and been with T-Mobile almost since the beginning and before that 30 years with sprint. Who the heck is running the show there? Does nobody answer to anyone?  I think it’s time to switch carriers. I hate T-Mobile do yourself a favor. Stay away!

I just wanted to say that T-Mobile is the worst phone company and will stand on the products that they sell. Back in November I switched to T-Mobile and got the internet service. I was told it was UNLIMITED. What I found out 2 weeks later is there is no UNLIMITED internet in St Louis with T-Mobile I ran out of data in 2 weeks.. I had customer service look that up for me. The phone that they sold to my boyfriend is the worst phone ever made. T-Mobile would not correct the problem or let him exchange the phone for one that would work properly. I switched to T-Mobile to lower my phone bill, but to my surprise, it ended up by higher. I was lied to from the very beginning. I decided to switch back to my old phone company just a few days ago. But I kept my phone that I had gotten from T-Mobile. The battery on that phone burned out. I went to T-Mobile to see if they would exchange the phone for one that worked. The salesperson and the so-called store manager (who was not Augusta) were rude and I was told it was not their problem. This is not customer service at all. You need to stand behind the product that you sell to people and stop lying about the products. The manager even agreed that the phone my boyfriend was sold is a low-end phone. We were never told that. I would never use the T-Mobile on 3554 S. Kingshighway Blvd. St Louis ever. That one star that I rated the is store should be a negative one star.

I went to the new TMobile store on 418 HWY 70 West in Havelock yesterday around 11, rightafter they opened.  The 3 employees had the best attitude,  smiles on their faces and were very kind and helpful.  You don’t find that a lot any more. Rarely.  I told them it was the best T Mobile experience I have ever had.

Given they've closed almost all physical stores they really need to increase they're online and over the phone support. Multiple times reach to their 1800 number only to ne told the wait would be approximately 5 minutes to then be put on hold for over 20 and never be helped.  Physical stores aren't Amy better, unless you're actually buy a phone, adding a plan etc. They do not care for it and just do the very minimum to help you and get you out the door.

Omg! What in the world has happened to this company? None English understanding people😳 can’t comprehend. Ridiculous prices. I’m over them. Please tell me where to go from here I gotta go. They make you very upset repeatedly saying the same stuff. Where are the English understanding people?? What happened Omg!!!! Bad customer service here horrible.  0 stars.

I tried multiple times in person (at the Trussville location sandwiched between racetrac and chevron) to pay the balance of my phone off and to return and cancel the lease on my husbands phone. The store manager was rude, tried to instigate a fight between himself and my grandson by leaning towards my grandson to put his face in his face and saying things to him that other around would not hear but we heard. Like when he complained that he did not like the way he was talking to his grandmother that we needed to pay off one phone and cancel the lease on another as my husband died February 13th but they would not do this transaction. He told my grandson, with his face almost touching his that my grandson was not going to do anything about it as his lips were trembling. My grandson told him his lips were trembling as he would like to lay one one him for being in his face and talking at me the way he was.  This manager refused to wait on us and told us to leave the property we were trespassing.  We left, this was not the first time he refused to let me pay for my phone so I could cancel my service with them and use a different carrier.  Then they called the police on us even though we were across the highway at a different store sitting and eating in front of their business.  The manager of Tmobile and his employees stood outside their business at laughed while smoking and having us warned that if we ever returned to Tmobile it would be a misdemeanor and we would be arrested, including my daughter who had not been present when we were told to leave she was in my truck going to pick up the phone I wanted to return to them I strongly suggest that you do no use this carrier service as they hold you hostage once they get you as a customer.

I recently set up a payment arrangement with T Mobile over chat I spoke to Two consumers they said it was noted and promised me no service interruption. This morning my service was interrupted. And then when trying to reconnect my service they tried to tell me my bill for June & July had went up 75 bucks and I made no changes to the plan.  Lucky I saved screen shots of all conversation after reaching about being charged more on my monthly bills.  All they could say is the additional money was for the reconnect charge for their error. This company is trying to rip off consumers by charging them more but cant explain why the bill is more. Why did Sprint sell to them. UGHH

My experience with T-Mobile customer service has been EXCELLENT!  Their team was attentive, knowledgeable and efficient in resolving my issues.  I am so happy I switched from Verizon…reception with T-Mobile is great…(no more issues than I experienced with Verizon)…Highly recommend switching 👍

The customer service agents are terrible.  Chis stressed me so bad that I ended up at the hospital with a miscarriage.  Because they were giving  me a run around  over there mistake. Rather than fixing. They sent phone to location. I went to location they said phone was not there. So I called customer via Chris he said phone was shipped back . I just dropped phone that day and was very upset  because I should of had replacement sent to me a month before. Which I was waiting with screen protector and case.  Kiki corporate rude 
Robert P rude
Chris terrible person all around
John terrible person  
Chris and John. And Robert  Said there was no manager or supervisor to speak with 
Nikki was the only good person in the whole center

I recently set up a payment arrangement with T Mobile but was unable to complete it being a monthly paid employee. Call T mobile thinking they could help me until I was paid. Once calling them she says oh it's an error on our end because in January you had a payment arrangement deleted. It was deleted because it was paid. How awful now that they are unable to help you. Please bring Sprint back your 20 plus years customers are suffering.

Decent service but they are shifting away from a consumer focus and have made a ton of bad calls. I am not sure how much more I will tolerate. 

They recently pushed out a new policy that only E-Check and debit cards will be allowed for the autopay discount when previously credit cards were allowed. This isn't too big of a deal but they have had multiple data breaches over the past 5 years including SSNs and financial info. With CCs at least you had extra fraud protection but it seems like they don't care and they just want to reduce their own costs.

Zero Stars. Profiteering grifters. 
T-Mobile offered a $100 discount on the new phone for a trade-in on the old phone. I asked if that was the case at the store and they said yes. What I didn’t know is that it’s incremental reduction on your new phone so you’re stuck for a little over 16 payments. The sleazy salesman also put a glass screen on when I thought I was getting the plastic screen, without giving me the two options. It cost more and is much harder, more slick, to navigate. Also, after I complained about the above my phone seems to loading slower.

I use to like tmobile. Not anyone. Customer service suck so bad. Tmobile say call from 7 to 7 youll get a dedicated team right here in the USA. Call now.people only Asians answering the phone. They dont understand anything and read from a script. I called over 20 times.for the same issue and no one can assist me. They give quick answer without explanations. They wont explain why im runnning into the issue im having. They dont even know. Guess it time to switch...the CEO needs to be fired. The old long hair guy was the best.

T-Mobile's 5G Home Internet Gateway, in my opinion, is a substandard product. I have had it for about 8 months now and found the speeds going down each month along with connectivity problems when connecting with the cell towers. Their tech support consists of untrained people that are not qualified to give useful advice. The company continues to add more people onto already overcrowded cell towers, which intensifies its already low broadband speeds. I do not recommend subscribing to T-Mobile due to their chronic problems with no plans of improving their long list of technical issues.

!5- minute wait for customer service and 50 minutes on the phone with no results. Also, they blame poor connections on high usage; it's always going out. They are quick to tell you that they do not guarantee coverage. So exactly what am I paying monthly for?

I ordered an iPhone SE a few weeks ago, and within a few days of me having the phone, it cuts off in the middle of me navigating the phone, and blinks the Apple logo. This has been a constant issue for the last several weeks, and the first T-Mobile representative that I spoke with, she was in the process of working to send me a replacement phone, the call dropped and the case closed. I speak with a chat representative while I'm at work, and she tells me she can't speak with me about my own account ! I call back later that evening, and the third representative, tells me to go to Best Buy instead! The local store rep tells me that there is nothing that they can do, and that I would need to contact Customer Care. I'm about sick of getting the run around, and I'm stuck here with a janky phone that they refuse to replace!! I will not be paying the bill for a phone I'm hardly able to use!!!

I wish I could give Zero Stars. T-Mobile has messed up my bill to the point that my bill is now $700. They added a non returned phone to my bill in November that the T-Mobile store did not return to the main office. Since January my phone has been turned off money added many different times to my account and then Representatives try to make me think that I should pay all of that. I have consistently paid over what my bill was every month and still they are claiming I owe them. Finally they turned my service off yesterday and will not turn it back on. The good thing is I no longer am going to stress over T-Mobile because I no longer will be a T-Mobile customer.

I bought a new iPhone but changed my mind and attempted to return it to the T-Mobile store I purchased from. The little 20 something weasels lied to me before I had made up my mind to buy, and told me they would provide a free screen protector if I bought the phone. When the phone arrived and I went back to the store, they denied the offer and said they "meant" I could by a screen protector when the phone arrived. A flat out lie. Not only did I return the new phone, I gave them the bums rush and switched to AT+T. I'm surprised they can stay in business lying to their customers.

I went by a T-Mobile store to sign up for 2 of the 55-Plus phone lines. The lines that are advertised to be $27.50/month for each line would have cost $110.00 per month for 2 lines, plus $30.00 per line for activation. Not exactly as advertised. The FTC should do something about their advertising, and T-Mobile should be ashamed.

They were very rude even the supervisor Jose N. when I wanted to cancel my service they refused to do so and said that they were keeping it active for another billing cycle and were gonna charge me the full month of service when I requested to cancel way before then. They did this just to make money and said they can not cancel until the end of the month even when I called to cancel ahead. The supervisor was rude, had no manners, and refused to put his supervisor on the phone he refused to do anything. The worst part about the treatment we got is that we were with this company for over 20 years and needless to say they did not care one bit. They are all about money and do not care about their customers.

On the date of 5.28.2023 I called the Tmobile customer care line requesting to remove features/plans from my account. The represent first stated it couldn’t be removed until the end of my billing cycle in which I told her that I knew this wasn’t true, she then told me this was protocol, I insisted that I knew this wasn’t true and I wanted them removed now. She then put me on hold and told me they were removed. I informed her that I should have received a notification about the removal in which I didn’t receive, she then put me on hold again and told me I should receive it now, I received one and told her I didn’t receive the other and she puts me on hold again and then I received the second notification. I thought my issue was resolved until I logged into my T-Mobile account on July 7th and noticed that the changes were never made, instead of removing the features she deactivated one of my lines. As I expressed my frustration at work regarding this matter I had two colleagues advised that they had similar situations occur and one of them advised that the reps “want to meet their quotas”. This is not the first time I’ve experienced this and I’m sure there are countless others who’ve experienced the same problem where the employees are intentionally not removing features from customers accounts as requested. When I spoke with someone from the executive team and asked them to listen to the call she said the call wasn’t recorded which I know this is NOT TRUE!!! They are making this a best practice to not remove features so their incentives aren’t negatively impacted and this behavior needs to stop!! And then to top it off when I spoke to an “executive specialist” she informed me she was going to remove the features and with the removal I’d have to pay for the cost of the Apple Watch in which I received as a “free gift” for being a “loyal” tmobile customer a year and a half ago!!!!! I never once paired the watch nor EVER USED IT!!!! I’ve made a complaint with the BBB!!! Their service is absolutely absurd!!!

I recently purchased a new iphone (14 pro max) and this entire process has been unnecessarily stressful. Apparently the 14 doesn't have a sim card slot. So i'd assume that apple would make this change for connivence. But, after arriving at the tmobile store to have them transfer my line, they tell me they couldn't because I HAVE to have a specific person do that for me (My phone plan is under my mom's business plan). It makes no sense to be because what if that person doesn't work for the company anymore? They're telling me theres really nothing they could do to help with the situation. I also did have to turn in the phone on the same day for differed trade-in because it was the last day. (couldn't do it earlier because of another variety if issues with the phone) The associate girl tells me "oh you can still do that but you wont have a device to use". Anyways, if I , I'd go start a new phone plan somewhere else.
